articles.title: Scientists say most people need more protein than current guidelines suggest

articles.title: Ebola and hantavirus can start like the flu but turn deadly fast

articles.title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope arrives in Florida ahead of SpaceX Falcon Heavy launch this summer
articles.summary: The Nancy Roman Grace Telescope has arrived at NASA's Kennedy Space Center for final preparations ahead of launching to join the orbital ranks of Hubble, Webb, Spitzer and Chandra.

articles.title: A private company will build and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 — and it's not SpaceX
articles.summary: A California-based company will design, build and launch a spacecraft for NASA's Aeolus mission to orbit around Mars, where the probe will be the first to provide daily measurements of the planet's global environment.

articles.title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches 24 Starlink satellites into low Earth orbit from California (video)
articles.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket carrying 24 Starlink satellites lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on Sunday, June 21, 2026.

articles.title: Hubble Details Early Galaxy Transforming Neighborhood
articles.summary: Astronomers using NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope have found something they never expected — ultraviolet light from a galaxy that existed just 1.4 billion years after the big bang.

articles.title: More trees can mean fewer birds, new study reveals

articles.title: T. rex took 40 years to reach full size, scientists find
articles.summary: Tyrannosaurus rex may have been a much slower grower than scientists realized. A new study of 17 tyrannosaur fossils found that the giant predator likely took about 40 years to reach its full size of roughly eight tons…

articles.title: Scientists open a million-year-old time capsule hidden beneath New Zealand

articles.title: Gas from Uranus reveals it has an icy centre
articles.summary: Carbon monoxide in Uranus's deep atmosphere indicates that the planet contains more ice than rock, suggesting it formed more like Neptune than we thought

articles.title: Almost the whole of Japan moved eastward after 2011 earthquake
articles.summary: An extremely unusual tectonic movement took place 15 minutes after the Tohoku earthquake in 2011, causing almost the whole of Japan to move 5 millimetres to the east

articles.title: Hidden dark force may slow cosmic structure growth, not speed it up
articles.summary: Dark matter is often portrayed as a cosmic loner, interacting with itself and the rest of the universe only through gravity. But what if dark matter particles also exert a hidden force on one another?

articles.title: Solid-state material turns visible light into high-energy UV at sunlight intensity, expanding solar energy potential
articles.summary: Two cups of warm water don't make one cup of boiling water. But in the quantum world, multiple low-energy photons can combine to produce a single, higher-energy photon.

articles.title: Espresso 'pucks' stop behaving predictably above certain pressures
articles.summary: When a physics student asked baristas at the Warsaw Coffee Conference what their biggest question for scientists was, the baristas said they wanted to know how to stop channeling during brewing.
